root_cause_historical_miss.summary: Calendly files were historically not in the DB because sync_links.py (pre-v5 upgrade) wrote ONLY markdown files — it had no DB write logic. The backfill process (content_backfill.py) DID scan the calendly folder (it scans all of Knowledge/content-library/), but the underlying ingest process likely failed silently due to schema mismatch.

primary_causes.cause: sync_links.py wrote files but not DB records (pre-W1.4)
primary_causes.evidence: W1.4 completion shows 'Option C - Embed dedicated DB upsert in sync_links.py' was the fix; this was a new capability
primary_causes.contribution: 100% of Calendly DB miss — no DB write ever happened

primary_causes.cause: content_library.py add() has schema drift: uses 'type' column but actual DB has 'content_type'
primary_causes.evidence: add() method: INSERT INTO items (id, type, ...) vs actual schema: content_type TEXT NOT NULL
primary_causes.contribution: Would cause ANY caller of lib.add() to fail with SQL error

primary_causes.cause: Backfill uses content_ingest.py which DOES use correct schema (content_type)
primary_causes.evidence: content_ingest.py create_record() correctly uses content_type; backfill would have worked IF sync_links wrote files at correct paths
primary_causes.contribution: Not a cause — backfill path is correct

historical_timeline: 1. sync_links.py (original) wrote markdown files to Knowledge/content-library/links/calendly/
2. sync_links.py did NOT create DB records — this was added in W1.4 upgrade
3. backfill would scan those files and attempt ingest via content_ingest.py
4. content_ingest.py correctly uses content_type, so backfill SHOULD have worked
5. However, if backfill was run BEFORE files existed, or if sync_links was never run, no files to backfill
6. W1.4 fixed the root cause by adding DB writes directly in sync_links.py
residual_issues: 7 legacy items exist from 'json_migration' source (vrijen_* IDs) that may be duplicates
content_library.py add() method is STILL broken (uses 'type' not 'content_type')

sync_model_spec.description: Reconcile filesystem content with DB, achieving a fixed-point state

step_1_scan_files.action: Scan CANONICAL_ROOT (Knowledge/content-library/) for all content files

step_2_scan_db.action: Query all items with non-null source_file_path
step_2_scan_db.query: SELECT id, source_file_path FROM items WHERE source_file_path IS NOT NULL AND deprecated = 0

step_3_diff.orphan_files: files in filesystem but NOT in DB (file_paths - db_paths)
step_3_diff.orphan_records: DB records where source_file_path no longer exists

orphan_files.action: Create DB record (if --repair) or flag for review
orphan_files.merge_aware: If file has frontmatter with id, check if id exists in DB first

orphan_records.action: Flag record with recommended action (never auto-delete)

idempotence_guarantee.invariant: Running sync twice with same filesystem/DB state produces 0 changes on second run
implementation: Step 3 diff uses set operations — deterministic
Step 4 reconcile checks record_exists before insert
Repair actions are additive only (no destructive ops without --force)
idempotence_guarantee.test: TC-IDEMPOTENCE: run sync, run sync again, assert report shows 0 orphan_files and 0 orphan_records

when_creating_from_orphan_file: Parse frontmatter for existing id field
If id exists in DB but with different source_file_path, this is a MOVE not a new record
If file has curated tags in frontmatter, preserve them (don't overwrite with defaults)
Set managed_fields to track which fields were auto-populated

cli_flags.--dry-run: Show what would happen without making changes
cli_flags.--repair: Automatically create records for orphan files (default: flag only)
cli_flags.--verify-content: Hash-check matched files for content drift

critical_fix_required.problem: Uses INSERT INTO items (id, type, ...) but actual schema column is 'content_type'
critical_fix_required.fix: Change 'type' to 'content_type' in the INSERT statement
critical_fix_required.notes: This is a bug fix, not a schema change. The method has been broken.

columns_to_add.name: subtype
columns_to_add.migration_sql: ALTER TABLE items ADD COLUMN subtype TEXT;
columns_to_add.index_sql: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_items_subtype ON items(subtype);
columns_to_add.use_case: Distinguish scheduling-link from generic link, profile from generic link, etc.

columns_to_add.name: summary
columns_to_add.migration_sql: ALTER TABLE items ADD COLUMN summary TEXT;
columns_to_add.use_case: Short AI-generated or curated summary (distinct from full content)

columns_to_add.name: managed_fields
columns_to_add.migration_sql: ALTER TABLE items ADD COLUMN managed_fields TEXT;
columns_to_add.use_case: JSON array tracking which fields were set by automation vs curated by human

managed_fields_design.semantics: Fields in this list were set by automation and CAN be overwritten by automation
managed_fields_design.not_in_list: Fields NOT in managed_fields are considered curated and must NOT be overwritten
managed_fields_design.frontmatter_sync: Optionally mirror in frontmatter as 'managed_by: automation' for transparency
managed_fields_design.precedence: DB is source of truth; frontmatter is for human readability

content_library_py.add_method: Fix to use content_type; add optional subtype, summary, managed_fields params

backward_compat.strategy: Keep 'type' as deprecated alias on ContentItem dataclass

repair_tool_design.description: Repair file↔DB inconsistencies found by sync

orphan_file_to_db.description: Create DB record for file that exists but has no DB entry
implementation: Parse file frontmatter for existing metadata
Detect content_type from path or frontmatter
Generate stable ID (or use frontmatter id if present)
merge_aware: If frontmatter has curated tags, preserve them
If frontmatter has id that exists in DB at different path, update path instead of creating new record

orphan_db_to_flag.description: Flag DB record where source_file_path no longer exists
implementation: Log to repair report with recommended action
recommended_actions: manual_review: File may have been moved — check for similar filename elsewhere
recreate_file: If DB record is valuable, regenerate file from content

path_mismatch_repair.description: DB record exists but source_file_path is wrong (file was moved)
path_mismatch_repair.detection: File at path A, DB record with id X points to path B, but path A has frontmatter id=X
implementation: Update source_file_path in DB to match actual file location

safety_guards: Never delete DB records without explicit --delete-orphans flag
Always produce structured report for audit
Dry-run is default (must pass --execute to make changes)

unit_tests.name: sync_idempotence
unit_tests.description: Run sync twice; second run should report 0 changes
unit_tests.setup: Create test DB and test files in temp dir

unit_tests.name: orphan_file_ingestion
unit_tests.description: File exists but not in DB; repair creates record
unit_tests.setup: Create file at Knowledge/content-library/links/test.md, ensure no DB record
unit_tests.assertion: DB record exists with correct source_file_path

unit_tests.name: curated_context_preservation
unit_tests.description: Repair does not overwrite curated tags
setup: Create file with frontmatter: audience: investors

unit_tests.name: orphan_record_flagging
unit_tests.description: DB record exists but file is missing; record gets flagged
unit_tests.setup: Create DB record pointing to non-existent file

unit_tests.name: path_mismatch_detection
unit_tests.description: File was moved but DB not updated
setup: Create file at path_A with frontmatter id=X
Create DB record with id=X pointing to path_B (old location)

integration_tests.name: full_calendly_parity
integration_tests.description: After sync, every Calendly file has a DB record
steps: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items WHERE source_file_path LIKE '%calendly%'

integration_tests.name: backfill_then_sync_stable
integration_tests.description: Running backfill then sync produces stable state

phase_1_fix_add_method.change: Fix add() to use content_type instead of type

phase_2_add_sync_command.recommendation: Option A — keep CLI unified, add sync logic to content_library.py

changes: ALTER TABLE items ADD COLUMN subtype TEXT
ALTER TABLE items ADD COLUMN summary TEXT
ALTER TABLE items ADD COLUMN managed_fields TEXT

notes_for_orchestrator: CRITICAL BUG: content_library.py add() method uses 'type' column but actual DB has 'content_type'. This should be fixed immediately — any caller of lib.add() will get a SQL error.
ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED: Calendly files weren't in DB because sync_links.py never wrote DB records (this was fixed in W1.4).
W1.4 FIXED THE IMMEDIATE ISSUE: Calendly sync now writes DB records. 18 files = 18 records. Parity achieved.
LEGACY DUPLICATES EXIST: 7 items from 'json_migration' source (vrijen_* IDs) may be duplicates of new calendly_* IDs. W2.2 should handle dedupe.
SCHEMA MIGRATION NEEDED: subtype, summary, managed_fields columns should be added (W1.2 spec). This is non-destructive.
REPAIR TOOL: Recommend creating repair subcommand that is dry-run by default and never deletes without explicit flag.
MERGE POLICY KEY: managed_fields column enables 'automation can update fields it set, but not curated fields'. This is the right pattern.
IMPLEMENTATION READY: This spec is detailed enough for a Builder to implement. No blocking questions remain.
